hook: You already reason logically every day — when you decide 'if it's raining, I'll take an umbrella' or spot a flaw in a friend's argument. But informal reasoning can be slippery and ambiguous, and mathematics demands precision. This unit gives you the tools to turn everyday reasoning into an exact, verifiable science — and unlocks the foundation of everything from algorithm design to mathematical proof.
concept: Every topic here is about one core idea: capturing reasoning itself as a formal system you can analyze and manipulate. In technical terms, this is the study of *propositional and predicate logic*, where truth becomes something you can compute and verify, not just feel.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to translate English statements into precise logical expressions using connectives like $\land$, $\lor$, and $\neg$, building the vocabulary of formal logic.
Master the technique of constructing truth tables to systematically determine the truth value of any compound proposition under every possible scenario.
Apply fundamental laws — such as De Morgan's Laws and distributivity — to simplify and transform logical expressions without changing their meaning.
You will learn to express statements about entire collections of objects using predicates and the universal ($\forall$) and existential ($\exists$) quantifiers.
Analyze and interpret complex statements involving multiple layered quantifiers, such as $\forall x\, \exists y\, P(x,y)$, and understand why their order fundamentally changes meaning.
You will evaluate whether arguments are logically valid by applying rules of inference — turning the intuition of 'good reasoning' into a rigorous, step-by-step process.
